summary: Backend: 26/26 pytest tests passed (auth, register all 3 roles, /me, logout, profile update, donor/hospital search w/ case-insensitive city, inventory CRUD + role protection, blood request flow, respond w/ inventory decrement, admin list/delete + self-protect, role-based access, analytics overview). Frontend: Landing page renders correctly with hero, 8 blood-group chips and 3 role cards. Login (admin@bloodbank.com/admin123) successfully redirects to /dashboard/admin. Recharts (7 wrappers) render demand/distribution/inventory charts. Welcome toast shown.

minor.issue: require_role(...) always allows admin via implicit override (line 168). Intentional but means admin bypasses hospital-only routes like /api/inventory/me. If a non-hospital admin hits PUT /api/inventory/me it would write to db.inventories with hospital_id=admin_id.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout).
secure=False on cookie. For production HTTPS this should be True (currently fine in preview).

integration_issues.issue: After clicking logout button, navigating to /dashboard/admin did NOT redirect to /login. Likely cause: logout clears the httpOnly cookie via API but does not clear the Bearer token stored in localStorage, so axios interceptor keeps sending Authorization header; OR the AuthContext does not invalidate the user state synchronously before navigation. Needs verification by main agent.

action_items: Verify the logout flow: ensure AuthContext.logout() clears Bearer token from localStorage AND user state, so ProtectedRoute redirects to /login. Also ensure axios interceptor stops sending stale token after logout.
Consider tightening require_role so admin override is explicit (e.g., separate is_admin check) to avoid admin accidentally writing to hospital inventory.
Optional: add brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login per playbook.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 461 lines — fine for now but consider splitting auth/inventory/requests/admin into routers as it grows.
require_role implicit admin override (line 168) silently grants admin access to any role-restricted endpoint; document clearly or refactor.
Frontend stores JWT in localStorage AND uses httpOnly cookie — pick one to avoid logout state drift; current dual approach is the likely root-cause of ProtectedRoute issue.
secure=False cookie; in production switch to secure=True with samesite='none' for cross-origin.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@bloodbank.com/admin123 (auto-seeded). Test users created during backend tests are cleaned up via admin DELETE.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create 1 donor + 1 hospital + 1 receiver (TEST_ prefix), then admin-delete all 3 in test_admin_delete_user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — all 26 pass. Frontend smoke covered: landing, admin login, charts. NOT yet covered via UI: full receiver→search→hospital request dialog flow, donor availability switch toggle, hospital inventory grid edit/save, donor approve/reject buttons. The likely logout/ProtectedRoute issue should be re-verified after fix by clicking logout then navigating to /dashboard/admin and confirming redirect to /login.
rca of the issue: ProtectedRoute fails to redirect after logout. Suspected RCA: AuthContext likely persists JWT in localStorage and api.js axios interceptor attaches it as Bearer header. The /api/auth/logout call only deletes the httpOnly cookie server-side; if the frontend logout handler does not also remove the localStorage token and clear React user state synchronously, the next render of ProtectedRoute may still see a valid user (or /api/auth/me still succeeds via Bearer header), so no redirect happens. Mitigation: in AuthContext logout, after awaiting the API call, clear localStorage('token') and setUser(null), then navigate('/login').
